commands.repair_reason: The remaining action is maintainer judgment on the public SDK alias metadata and optional live Telegram proof, not a concrete automated repair.; Cleared: The diff only changes TypeScript command registry/menu-building logic and focused tests; I found no concrete security or supply-chain regression. (sha=6b04de6ccdaaa4ee2b297740e95707f37104ae2c)
commands.review_summary:
The PR tags native alias command specs, budgets Telegram menu entries canonical-command-first with aliases only in leftover slots, strips alias metadata from registered menu payloads, and adds focused regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. for a source-level reproduction: current main expands nativeAliases into the native command spec list and applies the Telegram cap after that expansion. I did not run a live Telegram bot, but the linked issue includes a detailed live 2026.5.19 reproduction and the upstream Bot API documents the 100-command cap.
commands.review_followup: The remaining action is maintainer judgment on the public SDK alias metadata and optional live Telegram proof, not a concrete automated repair.

target.title: fix(telegram): preserve command slots for aliases
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #85199 by making Telegram native menu budgeting canonical-command-first:

- tag `NativeCommandSpec` / Telegram menu entries that come from `nativeAliases`
- register canonical commands before aliases, only including aliases when there is leftover Telegram menu budget
- strip the internal alias marker before calling Telegram `setMyCommands`
- keep typed alias routing unchanged (`/side` still routes to `/btw`)

This avoids a native alias consuming the 100th Telegram menu slot ahead of a real canonical command.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Telegram `nativeAliases` no longer consume menu slots before canonical commands. A 100-canonical-command menu plus `/side` alias keeps all 100 canonical commands visible, does not include `/side` in the full menu, and still routes typed `/side` to `/btw`.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS in `/Users/wuyangfan/Desktop/project/openclaw-work/openclaw`, running the patched PR head with Node and the repository TypeScript loader (`node --import tsx`). This directly exercised the patched registry and Telegram menu builder code paths in the real repository, not a mock.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'NODE'
import { buildCappedTelegramMenuCommands } from './extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-command-menu.ts';
import { listNativeCommandSpecs, normalizeCommandBody } from './src/auto-reply/commands-registry.ts';

const canonicalCommands = Array.from({ length: 100 }, (_, i) => ({
  command: `cmd_${i}`,
  description: `Command ${i}`,
}));
const allCommands = [
  ...canonicalCommands.slice(0, 99),
  { command: 'side', description: 'Alias', isAlias: true },
  canonicalCommands[99],
];
const capped = buildCappedTelegramMenuCommands({ allCommands });
const sideSpec = listNativeCommandSpecs().find((spec) => spec.name === 'side');
console.log(JSON.stringify({
  typedSideRoutesTo: normalizeCommandBody('/side what changed?'),
  sideNativeSpecIsAlias: sideSpec?.isAlias === true,
  registeredCount: capped.commandsToRegister.length,
  includesAliasInFullMenu: capped.commandsToRegister.some((command) => command.command === 'side'),
  includesLastCanonical: capped.commandsToRegister.some((command) => command.command === 'cmd_99'),
  totalCommandsForWarning: capped.totalCommands,
  overflowCount: capped.overflowCount,
}, null, 2));
NODE
```

**Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the patched OpenClaw checkout:

```json
{
  "typedSideRoutesTo": "/btw what changed?",
  "sideNativeSpecIsAlias": true,
  "registeredCount": 100,
  "includesAliasInFullMenu": false,
  "includesLastCanonical": true,
  "totalCommandsForWarning": 100,
  "overflowCount": 0
}
```

**Observed result after fix:** The patched menu builder registers exactly 100 commands, excludes the alias `/side` when there is no leftover budget, keeps the 100th canonical command (`cmd_99`), reports no overflow warning condition, and preserves typed `/side` dispatch by normalizing it to `/btw what changed?`.

**What was not tested:** Live Telegram `setMyCommands` / `getMyCommands` against a real bot token was not run from this PR branch. The proof exercises OpenClaw's patched registry and Telegram payload builder locally; the targeted tests cover the same cap and alias routing behavior.

## Verification

- `node --no-maglev ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/commands-registry.test.ts --pool=forks`
- `node --no-maglev ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-command-menu.test.ts --pool=forks`
- `node --no-maglev ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.test.ts --pool=forks`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm tsgo:test`
- `node node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-command-menu.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-command-menu.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.ts src/auto-reply/commands-registry.test.ts src/auto-reply/commands-registry.ts src/auto-reply/commands-registry.types.ts`
- `node node_modules/oxlint/bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/auto-reply/commands-registry.test.ts src/auto-reply/commands-registry.ts src/auto-reply/commands-registry.types.ts`
- `node node_modules/oxlint/bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-command-menu.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-command-menu.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Note: one combined 3-file Vitest invocation timed out locally after individual targeted suites were already passing; rerunning each targeted suite individually passed.
